newsSinceJun6: No company-specific fundamental news. ACMR caught in an early-June sector-wide semiconductor-equipment selloff (one ~9.4% down day plus -2.7% on June 9, roughly -12% from ~$89 over the past week) driven by near-term demand concerns and fresh downward analyst earnings-estimate revisions; bear-case scenarios now value the stock ~38% below current price. Minor SVP insider sale (~$1.59M, June 4-5 filing). Backdrop: strong Q1 beat (May 8, EPS $0.34 vs $0.28, rev $231.3M vs $217.8M) and a $150M follow-on equity offering completed in May.

sources.label: Simply Wall St - Bull case for ACMR could change after sector pullback and lower earnings estimates (Jun 9, 2026)
Alphastreet - ACM Research drops 9.4% amid sector-wide selling
Investing.com - ACM Research SVP Sotheara Cheav sells $1.59M in stock

verdicts.rankRationale: On the fab/HBM capacity lens, the new downward estimate revisions hit exactly the thesis's stated swing factor - order timing - so the sell-off is not purely flow-driven; but the structural exposure to AI-driven cleaning/plating capex is unchanged, so rank holds with a small negative drift.
verdicts.bull: Q1 beat (EPS $0.34 vs $0.28, revenue $231.3M vs $217.8M est.) with strong electrochemical-plating growth shows the AI/HBM capex thesis is executing; the $150M May raise funds capacity ahead of the cycle.
verdicts.neutral: A ~12% one-week drawdown roughly offsets the modest estimate cuts; the name remains levered to a capex cycle whose near-term cadence just got murkier, leaving risk/reward about where it was at yesterday's hold.
verdicts.bear: Sector-wide near-term demand concerns plus downward EPS revisions suggest order timing is slipping; with shares still +74.6% YTD and bear-case fair values ~38% below price, an estimate-cut cycle could compress the multiple hard.
verdicts.primaryCatalyst: Q2 orders/guidance confirming AI- and HBM-driven tool demand and order timing.
verdicts.primaryRisk: Continued analyst estimate cuts and China export-control escalation pushing orders out.

verdicts.rankRationale: On this crowding-sensitive alpha list, a sharp multi-day sector sell-off (-9.4% day, -2.7% today, ~-12% off the highs) with the niche process-complexity thesis intact deflates the post-rerating chase and modestly improves asymmetry; estimate trims are mild and sector-wide, not a thesis break.
verdicts.bull: The flush is taking momentum tourists out of a still under-followed $5.6B small-cap with a genuine cleaning/plating niche; fundamentals just beat in May, so the reset improves entry asymmetry.
verdicts.neutral: Estimate revisions are real but modest, and the geopolitical/China-policy overhang that caps the rank is unchanged; this is a small asymmetry improvement, not a re-thesis.
verdicts.bear: +74.6% YTD with a fresh $150M dilutive raise, insider selling, and downward revisions means the de-rating could run further; China revenue concentration remains the binary policy risk.
verdicts.primaryCatalyst: Stabilization of WFE sentiment plus next order/backlog datapoints validating the niche-share story at a reset valuation.
verdicts.primaryRisk: Deeper sector estimate-cut cycle or US-China export-control action hitting its China-centric customer base.
